title: Green-Wave Phasing: Coordinating a Traffic-Signal Grid
strategies: all-zero offsets: set every green phase to 0; all flow collapses to the DC center and every target corridor stays dark -- this reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline and scores ~0.1

grating superposition (phase-only): sum a linear phase ramp for each target corridor and keep only the resulting phase; steers substantial flow onto the corridors (efficiency ~0.75-0.8) but the phase-only truncation leaves them very unequal (uniformity ~0.4-0.5), so it beats the baseline yet leaves large headroom

Gerchberg-Saxton / iterative-Fourier-transform: alternately enforce the target amplitude on the corridors in the spectrum and unit amplitude on the grid, iterating to raise both efficiency and uniformity together -- clears the superposition heuristic but a phase-only device cannot reach the ideal, so it stays below 1.0

gradient ascent, random restarts, or simulated annealing on the composite metric: further squeeze uniformity, and adaptive weighting (e.g. boosting the dimmest corridors between iterations) trades a little efficiency for much better balance
